profile.primaryGoal: Reduce stress while staying productive and more aware of my body.
profile.focusAreas: Cycle tracking, habit consistency, study planning, sleep, emotional awareness
profile.supportNeeds: Keep guidance calm, practical, and specific. Suggest realistic next steps instead of too much at once.

profile.primaryGoal: Help learners connect body signals with safer daily habits.
profile.focusAreas: Cycle literacy, emotional awareness, session planning
profile.supportNeeds: Model compassionate coaching without giving medical advice.

profile.primaryGoal: Keep the platform safe, accessible, and respectful.
profile.supportNeeds: Clear signal and content review workflows.

logs.focus: Prepare for presentation and review class notes
Track patterns and adjust the schedule
Connect habits to my cycle phase

messages.text: I am Broom's AI copilot. I can summarize your profile, connect mood with cycle patterns, and turn your logs into realistic next steps.
What do you notice from my recent check-ins?
Your recent logs suggest stronger focus and energy in the last few days compared with the beginning of the cycle. You also seem to benefit from calmer planning on lower-energy days.
How would community support and mentorship fit into my experience?
Broom can pair your personal tracking with a safer anonymous community, verified mentors, and stronger AI guardrails so support feels more human and trustworthy.
Give me one simple way to plan rest days.
Try blocking one recovery window after your lower-energy day and set a single small habit, like a soothing tea or a short mindfulness break.

communityPosts.body: I noticed I study better a few days after my period. Does anyone else plan difficult work around that window?
When cramps and deadline pressure happen together, what small habits help you stay calm without overloading yourself?
This sample post has been temporarily hidden after two reports so an admin can review it before any stronger action is taken.
How do you keep your community posts anonymous but still get useful feedback?

communityGuidelines.title: Protect anonymity
communityGuidelines.copy: Use aliases, avoid sharing direct contact details, and keep private medical or personal identifiers out of public threads.

communityGuidelines.title: Report first, ban later
communityGuidelines.copy: One or two reports should temporarily hide a post for review. Final action belongs to an admin, not crowd voting alone.

communityGuidelines.title: No harmful advice
communityGuidelines.copy: Medical misinformation, harassment, sexual content, and pressure tactics should trigger fast review and repeat-offender escalation.

communityGuidelines.title: Nudge positive behavior
communityGuidelines.copy: Posting prompts should remind users to be respectful, avoid diagnosis, and support others without attacking them.

moderationQueue.title: Temporary hide threshold
moderationQueue.copy: One to two credible reports can hide content from the main feed while admins review context and user history.

moderationQueue.title: Human decision
moderationQueue.copy: Admins can restore, warn, mute, suspend, or ban depending on severity and repeat behavior patterns.

moderationQueue.title: Repeat offender policy
moderationQueue.copy: Accounts with repeated harmful posts move from warnings to stronger restrictions instead of instant permanent bans.

mentors.name: Arielle N.
mentors.focus: Career confidence, coding routines, interview prep

mentors.name: Dr. Teta U.
mentors.focus: Cycle education, wellbeing habits, health literacy
mentors.pricing: Paid sessions plus free group Q&A

mentorshipAccess.title: Mentor verification
mentorshipAccess.copy: Mentors apply, complete identity checks, and are approved by admins before they can guide users.

mentorshipAccess.title: Training and boundaries
mentorshipAccess.copy: Training covers communication skills, safeguarding, privacy, and how to avoid acting like a therapist or clinician.

mentorshipAccess.title: Accessibility model
mentorshipAccess.copy: Mentorship can be paid while still offering trials, sponsored sessions, or scholarship access for users who cannot pay.

aiGuardrails.title: No diagnosis
aiGuardrails.copy: The assistant should never make absolute clinical claims. It uses language like may, might, and could be linked to.

aiGuardrails.title: Feedback and review
aiGuardrails.copy: Users can flag misleading answers, and repeated reports should trigger prompt updates or human review of assistant behavior.

aiGuardrails.title: Context-aware caution
aiGuardrails.copy: The model can personalize using goals, cycle signals, and habits, but it should stay advisory rather than authoritative.

crisisProtocol.title: Detect alarming language
crisisProtocol.copy: Messages about self-harm, abuse, or severe distress should stop normal coaching and switch to a safer response mode.

crisisProtocol.title: Respond with care
crisisProtocol.copy: The assistant should acknowledge distress, avoid judgment, and encourage immediate support rather than trying to solve the crisis alone.

crisisProtocol.title: Show help resources
crisisProtocol.copy: The experience should surface relevant support contacts, trusted adults, or local crisis resources when available.
